channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Belgian Football Team Mocks Trump in Locker Room Celebration After Victory Over U.S.
item.description: Belgium's national football team, the Red Devils, mocked U.S. President Donald Trump in a locker room celebration after defeating the United States in a politically charged World Cup 2026 match. Video...

item.title: Leaders condemn China's missile test and discuss regional security ties
item.description: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and Solomon Islands leader Matthew Wale have jointly condemned China's recent submarine-launched missile test as 'provocative and destabilising', highlightin...

item.title: Nairobi Police Enhance Security Checkpoints Ahead of Saba Saba Commemoration
item.description: The National Police Service (NPS) has deployed extensive security checkpoints across Nairobi to ensure law and order during the annual Saba Saba commemoration on July 7, 2026. The measure aims to prev...

item.title: Supreme Court Rules on Admissibility of Electronic Evidence in Duterte Impeachment Trial
item.description: The Supreme Court is addressing the admissibility of electronic copies as evidence in the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte. Lawyers for both the prosecution and defense clashed over th...

item.title: Daimler Enterprises Directors Face Charges Over Land Fraud
item.description: Detectives from the Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) have arrested John Mugo Njeru and Irima Mugo, directors of Daimler Enterprises Limited, over an 8.35-acre land fraud scheme along Old M...

item.title: Chennai fisherfolk's protest against market relocation sparks solidarity
item.description: The excerpt from 'Sea Goddess' by Akila Kannadasan follows the lives of Semba and Maari, two fisherwomen in Chennai battling against government plans to relocate their traditional fish market. Anbu, a...

item.title: Heat-related health impacts persist as Canada recovers from recent heatwave
item.description: Canadians are experiencing lingering health effects after a recent heatwave, with experts describing the phenomenon as a 'heat hangover.' The term, popularized on social media, refers to symptoms like...

item.title: South Sudan's peace agreements perpetuate war economies through elite control
item.description: The article explores how South Sudan's political elite have maintained power by integrating war economies into peace agreements. Despite formal independence in 2011, the country has experienced repeat...

item.title: Tanzania Aims to Revamp DITF into Global Innovation and Trade Hub by 2050
item.description: The Tanzanian government, through Vice President Emmanuel Nchimbi, has outlined a vision to transform the Dar es Salaam International Trade Fair (DITF) from a traditional product exhibition into a glo...

item.title: Bafana star's father urges PSL clubs to nurture local talent like Relebohile Mofokeng
item.description: Relebohile Mofokeng, the 21-year-old Bafana Bafana star who recently joined Belgian side Royale Union Saint-Gilloise, has left a lasting impact on South African football. His father, Sechaba, expresse...

item.title: Young Australians navigating singlehood: Trends, challenges and shifting social norms
item.description: The article explores the rising trend of young Australians remaining single for extended periods, examining factors like technology's role in replacing face-to-face interaction, evolving mental health...

item.title: IOC Monitors Sarakasi Election Amid Leadership Uncertainty
item.description: The International Olympic Committee (IOC) continues to oversee the disputed elections of Tanzania's Olympic Committee (TOC) following a contentious voting process in Dar es Salaam. Dr. Donald Rukare, ...

item.title: Senate Orders Probe Into Xenophobic Attacks Against Nigerians in South Africa
item.description: The Nigerian Senate has directed its Committee on Foreign Affairs to investigate renewed xenophobic attacks against Nigerians in South Africa, following intense debates among lawmakers. Senators conde...

item.title: Kerry Martin's Baby Mama Grieves After Child's Death
item.description: Faith Wanjiku, the baby mama of influencer Kerry Martin, has shared her heartbreak after losing her child. The 27-week premature birth led to severe medical complications, leaving Wanjiku with a KSh 5...

item.title: New York City Reports 23 Legionnaires' Disease Cases as Health Officials Investigate Upper East Side Outbreak
item.description: New York City health officials are investigating an outbreak of Legionnaires' disease centered in parts of the Upper East Side after the number of confirmed cases rose to 23 as of July 6. Seventeen pa...

item.title: Rising Grocery Prices Drive Canadians to Adopt 'Pay Later' Options
item.description: A growing number of Canadians are turning to 'pay later' payment options as grocery prices continue to climb, according to a report by an app that tracks consumer spending habits. The trend highlights...

item.title: Tanzania Police Strengthen Security Measures at Sabasaba Trade Fair
item.description: The Tanzania Police Force has assured the public that comprehensive security measures have been implemented at the Dar es Salaam International Trade Fair (DITF) and across the country to ensure the sa...

item.title: South Africa's Path to Sustainable Energy Independence
item.description: The article highlights South Africa's proactive approach to building sustainable energy resilience, with the Western Cape leading the way towards achieving energy independence from Eskom by 2035. Key ...

item.title: Cotabato's Indigenous Crafts Sustained Through Government Initiatives
item.description: The provincial government of Cotabato, led by Governor Emmylou Taliño-Mendoza, has launched a program to sustain indigenous crafts and trades that provide income for marginalized communities. A recent...

item.title: Lessons from Norway's youth sports success for Australia
item.description: The article explores Norway's unique approach to youth sports, which emphasizes enjoyment over competition and has led to international success. Despite a population of just 5.6 million—about the size...
